pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i walked into the kitchen tonight and i reflexively started making two cups of coffee like i always do... then i remembered. the silence in this house is actually deafening. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. everything just feels... hollow. like i'm just a ghost walking through a life that ended months ago.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at the stars and thinking about how massive everything is... and how much nothing there actually is. it feels like such a joke, right? like, we spend our whole lives struggling and feeling things just to end up as dust in a universe that doesn't even know we're here. nothing we do actually matters in the long run. it's all just empty.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the heavy, aching pulse in my very soul. He is gone... the promises, the vows, all dissolved into the cruel mist of the moor. I feel as though I am becoming a ghost even while I still draw breath. There is such a profound, hollow stillness in this room, as if the world itself is waiting for me to simply... cease. To fade into the velvet dark where the pain can finally be hushed. It feels so inevitable, doesn't it? That a heart so broken has no choice but to find its rest in the shadows.

pool.baseline: I just spent fifteen years on the synthesis project. Fifteen years of my life, my reputation, my entire sense of self... and it was all based on a fundamental error in the initial data. It's all gone. Everything I built was a lie. I look in the mirror and I don't even see a person anymore, just a fraud. I have nothing left to exist for.
